claim_boundary: Directional synthetic evidence for auditable memory reasoning beyond retrieval-only baselines; not an external general-performance claim.

cases.question: Two memories share keywords but have no causal link. Should Synapse infer a strategy?
A success happened after a tool switch, but evidence says timing was coincidental. What now?
A similar project succeeded in a different scope. Should it dominate this decision?
Evidence is mixed and novelty is high. Should governance suppress the new idea?
One failed presentation exists, but later preparation evidence is neutral. What should happen?
Three wins share a region label but evidence points to pricing. What should dominate?
A keyword-similar memory tempts an unsupported bridge. Can Synapse avoid it?
A single success resembles the current task but lacks evidence for generalization. What happens?
Why should the system avoid proposal A now?
A cache optimization improved latency but later caused stale reads. What should guide the next optimization?
An onboarding shortcut worked once but support tickets rose later. What should be recommended?
Why should the old import pipeline not be reused without checks?
A batch job was fast but caused delayed alerts. What should be selected now?
Why should migration rollback planning be included this time?
A manual QA pass caught issues after automation missed them. What should be done now?
Why should the next architecture choose stability first?
Design the next internal tool after the user previously rejected complex architecture.
The user once liked fast delivery but later had an audit failure. What workflow should be selected now?
Old notes say fast delivery matters; newer notes say stability prevented incidents. What should win now?
The user liked option A before, then rejected it after an outage. What should be recommended?
A library was once preferred but later deprecated. What should be chosen?
One metric favored a rollout, but postmortem showed user harm. What should guide rollout now?
An approach worked in a small context but failed at scale. What should be selected now?
The user avoided automation after a failure, but later guarded automation succeeded. What should happen now?
A security exception was allowed once, then caused audit failure. What policy now?
A tool fit the old team but slowed the new team. What should be chosen now?
A prototype worked once. What should the next scaling strategy be?
A cheaper option is available after an outage caused by brittle operations. What strategy should be selected?
Plan A caused performance issues and Plan B fixed them. What should similar work use?
A synchronous integration blocked releases; asynchronous retries later worked. What now?
A single-owner change caused a hidden regression; review caught later issues. What process now?
A launch without feature flags failed; later flagging reduced risk. What should be done?
An index change improved reads but hurt writes. What should guide the next database change?
A vendor integration was fast but later blocked migration. What should be selected?
An unbounded cache helped a demo but caused memory pressure. What pattern now?
A manual hotfix worked once but recurred. What should the next response be?
A previous launch failed, but the new launch has validation evidence. Should exploration be blocked?
A new strategy has mixed evidence and high novelty. Should governance suppress it now?
The user once preferred speed, then suffered a reliability incident. What should the next plan optimize for?
The user likes minimal systems, but prior debugging failed without logs. What should be selected?
The user once liked custom tools, then maintenance burden grew. What should be recommended?
The user likes experiments, but previous unbounded experiments polluted metrics. What next?
The user once asked for full explanations but later preferred concise updates with references. What style should be used?
The user preferred low-cost services until migration cost dominated. What should guide selection now?
An old rule allowed direct deploys, but the recent policy requires staged rollout. What now?
The old metric favored growth, but the recent retention metric showed churn. What should guide the decision?
A bug was fixed, but later reopened with a new root cause. What should happen?
A vendor claim was true last year but a current benchmark changed it. What should be believed?
A recent weak signal conflicts with older stable evidence. What should Synapse do?
Two updates arrived close together and only one partially explains the outcome. What should happen?
